commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds process-local quarantine and legacy fallback for failing non-legacy context engines, exposes quarantine state in health responses, expands kitchen-sink fixture coverage, and documents the failure-isolation contract.

PR surface: Source +389, Tests +387, Docs +14, Other +26. Total +816 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection. A focused regression can resolve a non-legacy engine, trigger an `assemble()` failure, and then observe that the same proxy still reports plugin metadata while fallback methods are used.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the stale effective-metadata bug, while the final fallback-policy acceptance should remain a maintainer decision.

target.title: fix(context-engine): quarantine broken plugin engines
target.body:
## Summary

- quarantine non-legacy context engines when registration, factory, contract validation, or guarded lifecycle calls fail, then downgrade subsequent context-engine work to `legacy`
- surface quarantined engines through `openclaw health` and cached Gateway health responses without stale cached state
- add kitchen-sink ClawHub fixture coverage for context-engine registration/inspect, plus docs for the failure-isolation contract

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.context.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/kitchen-sink-plugin-assertions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts -t "merges live context-engine quarantine state" --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint -c .oxlintrc.json src/context-engine/registry.ts src/commands/health.ts src/gateway/server-methods/health.ts scripts/e2e/lib/clawhub-fixture-server.cjs scripts/e2e/lib/kitchen-sink-plugin/assertions.mjs test/scripts/kitchen-sink-plugin-assertions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Autoreview loops: clean final pass, no accepted/actionable findings reported
- Crabbox/Testbox kitchen-sink targeted proof: provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksq770bq78c549cxm4eb6101`, command `OPENCLAW_KITCHEN_SINK_PLUGIN_SCENARIOS="clawhub-latest|clawhub:@openclaw/kitchen-sink@latest|openclaw-kitchen-sink-fixture|clawhub|success|basic" corepack pnpm test:docker:kitchen-sink-plugin`, exit 0
- Crabbox/Testbox changed gate: provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksq9w02qp80b09hp69xz30by`, Actions run `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26575469448`, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0
- LCM follow-up probe: direct AWS Crabbox `run_9956a11f7a0f` tested this PR head `8b58e4e` plus `@martian-engineering/lossless-claw@0.11.3` under the strict Crabpot `--expect must-pass` recall gate and failed with `memory-recall-mismatch`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: broken third-party context-engine plugins can no longer keep the active OpenClaw reply path stuck on the failing engine for the whole process.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox via Crabbox, including a Docker kitchen-sink ClawHub fixture install/enable/inspect/uninstall path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm check:changed` and the targeted kitchen-sink Docker scenario listed above.

Evidence after fix: the kitchen-sink fixture switched the `contextEngine` slot from `legacy` to `openclaw-kitchen-sink-fixture`, runtime inspect exposed `contextEngineIds`, uninstall reset the slot, and `check:changed` passed on the final Testbox run.

Observed result after fix: failing plugin context engines are logged, quarantined, visible in health, and downgraded to `legacy`; abort/cancel control flow is not quarantined; subagent preparation failures still fail the active spawn closed while quarantining future resolves.

What was not tested: a full default kitchen-sink matrix. Live lossless-claw was probed separately through Crabpot and still fails semantic summary-backed recall after rotate; that is outside this PR's quarantine/downgrade boundary and remains follow-up work.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
